indicators.name: couch
indicators.rationale: The presence of a couch suggests a living area rather than a dedicated workspace.

indicators.name: table
indicators.rationale: A dining table is not suitable for computer work due to its height and lack of ergonomics.

indicators.name: grass
indicators.rationale: Outdoor grass is not conducive to focused work and can be distracting.

indicators.name: potted plant
indicators.rationale: A potted plant is a decorative element, not a functional workspace feature.

indicators.name: closed door
indicators.rationale: A closed door can provide some privacy, though it is not a dedicated workspace feature.

indicators.name: low-traffic layout
indicators.rationale: The layout is not cluttered and does not have high-traffic areas, which can be distracting.

reasoning_summary: The scene is marginally adequate for computer work (Solo). While there is a closed door for privacy and a low-traffic layout, the presence of a couch, table, grass, and potted plant suggest this is more of a living area than a dedicated workspace. The lack of a flat work surface and ergonomic seating makes it challenging to set up a productive workstation.
